Title: OsAAP6 functions as an important regulator of grain protein content and nutritional quality in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Amino acid permease (AAP) family; APC superfamily; PF01490 consensus domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Grain protein content; grain storage proteins (glutelins, prolamins, globulins, albumins); total amino acids
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves cereal grain nutritional quality and dietary protein supply for humans
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of grain protein content and nutritional quality; increases grain storage proteins and total amino acids, increases amylose, decreases starch and gel consistency
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning of qPC1; overexpression; complementation; RNAi knockdown; co-segregation analysis; qRT-PCR expression correlation; promoter deletion-GUS assay; amino acid uptake assay; stem sap amino acid analysis; transmission electron microscopy; subcellular localization in protoplasts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPC1 was fine-mapped to a 6.7-kb region containing a single gene, OsAAP6. 2. Overexpression and complementation increased grain protein content, while RNAi reduced it, confirming OsAAP6 as the causal gene. 3. Higher OsAAP6 expression correlated with higher grain protein content and increased glutelins, prolamins, globulins, albumins, and total amino acids. 4. OsAAP6 enhanced root uptake of multiple amino acids and altered amino acid distribution through stems, flag leaves, and hulls. 5. Natural variation in the 5' regulatory region, especially cis-element polymorphisms in the 5'-UTR/promoter, was associated with expression differences and GPC diversity mainly in indica cultivars. 6. OsAAP6 localized to the ER and was highly expressed in endosperm and vascular/root tissues.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all examined tissues; highest in endosperms at 5-12 days after flowering; strong in vascular tissues of hull, root, pulvinus, internode, node, seed and endosperm; mainly in root rhizodermis and phloem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript levels in NIL(ZS97) endosperm than NIL(NYZ); strongest at 5-12 DAF endosperm; GUS strong in vascular tissues and endosperm during grain filling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials over 2 years; grain filling stage (5-12 DAF, 10 DAF); amino acid uptake assay in 20-amino-acid solution under controlled growth chamber conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Natural variation sequencing of OsAAP6 genomic region in 197 rice mini-core accessions; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased GPC was coupled with increased amylose and reduced starch content and gel consistency; no significant effects on plant morphology, flowering time, grain size, or grain yield reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving rice grain protein content and nutritional quality without reported yield penalty; favorable high-expression alleles and promoter haplotypes can be selected in breeding, especially in indica; overexpression or promoter engineering of OsAAP6 may enhance grain protein and amino acid content in rice and potentially other cereals
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native OsAAP6 promoter from ZS97 for complementation and GUS deletion assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsAAP6 corresponds to QTL qPC1 controlling natural variation in grain protein content. Coding-region differences between ZS97 and NYZ were not protein-altering except one synonymous mutation; regulatory variation in promoter/5'-UTR appears responsible for expression and phenotype differences. OsAAP6 is classified here as a Common Gene because it functions primarily as an amino acid transporter rather than an enzyme or transcription factor.
